steps.title: Read the intersection point from the graph
steps.content: The absolute value function (V-shaped curve, vertex at $(-3, 4)$) and the linear function (the line, passing through $(0, 8)$) intersect on the left branch at approximately x = −3.5 and y = 4.5.

steps.title: Express as fractions and eliminate wrong choices
steps.content:
Convert the decimal coordinates to fractions:
$$x = -3.5 = -\frac{7}{2}, \quad y = 4.5 = \frac{9}{2}$$
  • Choice A: $(0, 8)$ — that is the y-intercept of the line, not the intersection point. ✗
  • Choice B: Has the wrong sign on the x-coordinate — does not lie on both functions. ✗
  • Choice C: $\left(-\frac{7}{2},\, \frac{9}{2}\right)$ — verify: $\left|-\frac{7}{2}+3\right|+4 = \frac{1}{2}+4 = \frac{9}{2}$ ✓ and $-\frac{7}{2}+8 = \frac{9}{2}$ ✓. This lies on both curves. ✓
  • Choice D: $(-3, 4)$ — that is the vertex of the absolute value function, not the intersection. ✗
The solution to the system is $\left(-\frac{7}{2}, \frac{9}{2}\right)$. That matches choice C — C is correct.
